pages/about-me.html.title: About Me
pages/about-me.html.description: Im just a guy who lives in Milwaukee. My hobbies include typing, eating chips, regretting how many chips i've eaten, and other things. I own a lot of tools that I would love to use to build things, so let me know if that's you! I would also love to see something you built, so definitely reach out to me if you've done something cool. But not too col otherwise I might get jealous. Follow me on Bluesky (its not just for nerds anymore) (just kidding it absolutely is)I also exist in the real world, but you will not find me You can also email me at brennanj@proton.me

headers.heading: GO

pages/blog-posts.html.title: Blog Posts
pages/blog-posts.html.description: Posted on March 4th, 2026Another big update everyone. I am going to be a father soon. I have a few pressing questions: 1. How do you ensure that none of your erraticisms get passed down to your offspring 2. At what stage can he start holding the light for me? That's actually it as far as questions go. I'm sure I'll have more once I get into it ~Brennan, soon to be a father~Posted on December 7th, 2025Big update everyone. The task that I have been griping about for the last few months has been completed. I have posted some photos of it in the gallery but it looks great and I am proud of myself. It turns out that I, in fact, CAN do things. I am capable of greatness. And by greatness I mean fairly easy tasks done pretty well. Hire me to perform the tasks that are impossible ~Robot Brennan~Posted on September 2nd, 2025Hey hotties, it's another message from your favorite guy. Sorry, not Richard Segway, inventor of the segway; it's just me, Brennan. I have been working on building a lovely little reading nook for my dear friends. And boy oh boy is it taking forever. I have now told them twice that it's "only going to be a few more hours." I'm getting desperate, the work is not wmoving at the speed that I want, and I am beginning to think that they don't believe it will ever get done (this could be in my head). They keep saying things like "stop apologizing" and "it's really no trouble, we're in no rush", but I can read BETWEEN THE LINES. So my life advice for anybody aspiring to not value their time and help their friends, don't promise anything. Say that it will take one thousand years. Say that the winds of time will sweep any remnant of you under mountains of sandy loam before this nook is done. Tell them that their great great great great grand-robot-children will LOVE this keepsake. Signing off, your friendly neighborhood slowpoke, ~Brennan Jehovah Reynolds~Posted on August 15th, 2025I have been rocked by 3 inches of water in my basement. This is no cause for alarm as I have a contingency plan, standing in 3 inches of water and staring at it. Since this also didn't work, we were forced to remove all the tiles, mop everything, purchase a mongo dehumidifier (short out menards for having some, and I take that shoutout back for your political affiliations), and get to WORK Needless to say I am tired. I am working on a project soon with my dear friend Hayden and I cannot wait to get that underway. Just gotta get through all the dumb stuff that Mother nature (that cruel temptress) has in store for me. Oh! Also! Mother Nature has taken my car! Rude! Signing off, your lover and handyman, ~Brennan Reynolds~Posted on March 20st, 2025Exciting news everyone. I have done electrical work in the basement. Several outlets and so many sconces (yeah that's right im a fancy man). And guess what? There is not one fire currently burning in the basement. If you need electrical work, I legally cannot help you but I can put a bunch of wires all over your basement and maybe sometimes power will go through them and to a thing you want and NOT the air or a flammable blanket.Posted on January 20th, 2025A new year and a new me. Just kidding its the same old bullshit. I made a cool Matcha stand, and im going to be helping some friends with some projects (aka farming them for content). I am looking forward to posting more stuff here and becoming so good at woodworking. Real talk though, I plan on fighting harder to stay out of time-sinks and doomscrolls. I am trying to make the choice that benefits my mental health. Am I succeeding? Absolutely notPosted on December 21st, 2024Hi there. I made a sound system for a basement with a mixer so that you can hear two different tv's AND a bluetooth device all together and invidually control volume and equalizer for each device I don't want to brag but im incredible and I can do it for you tooPosted on December 2nd, 2024Hi guys. I have a website. And soon I will make a logo. And after that a business card, and then after that maybe i'll actually get to work and do something for once in my lifePosted on November 29th, 2024Well folks, the time has come. It is time that I build a website to channel all my energy to. This will undoubtedly be a waste of time, but fuck it lets rumblePosted on September 35th, 2045Test post delete immediately DO NOT LET THIS GET ONTO YOUR SITE~Robot Brennan~

headers.heading: Do you think a baby will get in the way of me building things?
Whenever you estimate a job, multiply that number by 3
The floodwaters have decimated my basement
I have defeated the electric gremlins in my basement

pages/client-testimonials.html.title: Client Testimonials
pages/client-testimonials.html.description:
"Brennan pitched, designed, and installed a magnificent sound system for my newly constructed basement bar. I did not have a plan for my audio set up at that point, so he actually solved a problem for me before I even realized that I HAD a problem. Now that ’s some serious truth-in-advertising from iwilltrytosolveyourproblems.com! I highly recommend using Brennan for the next time you need a problem solved. Just give him a call and BADDA BING BADDA BOOM, he’ll be good to go!” - Joseph Kay, upon completion of a perfect basement bar sound system Procurement and getting yelled at by salespeople at a big company somewhere "5/5, wait oh no I have to think of a whole thing? Oh no this is stressful" - Hayden Heun, upon being assisted with a flat tire Engineer at a place somewhere "The first one was not what I wanted. But, once he actually looked at the photos I sent him and listended to what I was telling him, it turned out great" Elena LASTNAMEREDACTED, upon receiving an AWESOME Matcha Stand works at school taking kid's vapes away brennan1.pngbrennan2.pngbrennan4.pngWould you like to share your experience with Brennan Solution Company? Contact us to add your testimonial! Email:
brennanj@proton.me
I would give you my phone number too but my wife said I "shouldn't have that on the internet where anyone can get it"brennan3.png

pages/projects.html.title: Projects
pages/projects.html.description:
A list of Projects that I have (more or less) succeeded on, arranged from oldest to newestHayden and Amanda's Reading Nook:
Joe's Sound System:
Ellie's Matcha Stand:
Brennan's Sweet Project Workbench:

projects/brennan-basement_workbench/bren.title: Brennan's Sweet Project Workbench
projects/brennan-basement_workbench/bren.description: This desk is cool because if I don't work on any projects it slowly piles up with stuff that has to go somewhere else. That way, whenever I get the drive to get to work and start a project, I end up having to spend 15 minutes cleaning off this desk! Do I still have motivation to start something after I spent a bunch of time looking at everything I haven't done yet? Not often! Magic!

projects/ellie-matcha_stand/ellie's-matc.title: Ellie's Matcha Stand
projects/ellie-matcha_stand/ellie's-matc.description: When my perfect and incredible wife asked me to make a little stand for matcha equipment I said yes of course and got to work. Because that's what you do as a husband, you DELIVER. I'm not being kept around this house for my charms and good looks, let me tell you. I am being kept around because I am in a strong relationship built on trust, friendship, and love, and also because I DELIVER. This was also a fun project because I got to use dowels for the first time. We learn and we grow, AND DELIVER

projects/h-a-nook/hayden-and-amanda's-re.title: Hayden and Amanda's Reading Nook
projects/h-a-nook/hayden-and-amanda's-re.description: An absolutely stunning nook made out of the finest construction material I found at Menards. It sits peacefully in an architecurally bizarre part of their house and now it simply BEGS you to sit and read on itThey were deeply happy and although I have never been invited back into their home I assume they are using it to this day

projects/joe-sound_system/joe's-sound-sy.title: Joe's Sound System
projects/joe-sound_system/joe's-sound-sy.description: When Joe asked me to build this sound system I said "what does it do??" When he explained to me that sound is recorded using magnets and then reproduced using the ELECTRONS TO DRIVE AN ELECTROMAGNET I nearly died laughing. but I did some reading and turns out, he was right. This was a drawer in his basement bar and we fit in a mixer, amp, and bluetooth receiver so he can use his ipod (yeah, this nerd still has an ipod) to play music, or he can listen to whatever is on his tv (yeah, this guy has a tv), or he can DO BOTH with the power of MIXINGHe loves it and I loved doing it

index.html.title: index
index.html.description:
Hi! My name is Brennan and you've found my website. I love to build things and help my friends with projects, so I figured I would make a place on the internet where I can show you what I've done. If you see anything here you don't like, please don't tell me about it. I would love to work on something with you, email me at brennanj@proton.meNow lets see some stuff!
Blog Posts
Links
Projects
About Me
What People say about me

info_showCustomIcons.description: Show custom icons for files and folders

info_showDefaultFolderIcons.description: Show a default icon of a folder for every folder in the tree

info_showDefaultFileIcons.description: Show a default icon of a file for every file in the tree

info_defaultFolderIcon.description: The icon to use for folders. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultFileIcon.description: The icon to use for files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultMediaIcon.description: The icon to use for media files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_exposeStartingPath.description: Whether or not to show the current file in the file tree when the page is first loaded

info_minCollapseDepth.description: Only allow outline items to be collapsed if they are at least this many levels deep in the tree.

info_type.description: Will this feature be placed before, after, or inside (at the beggining or end).

info_featurePlacement.description: Where to place this feature on the page. (Relative to the selector)

info_displayTitle.description: Descriptive title to show above the feature

info_showOrphanNodes.description: Show nodes that are not connected to any other nodes.

info_showAttachments.description: Show attachments like images and PDFs as nodes in the graph.

info_allowGlobalGraph.description: Allow the user to view the global graph of all nodes.

info_allowExpand.description: Allow the user to pop-out the graph view to take up the whole screen

info_attractionForce.description: How much should linked nodes attract each other? This will make the graph appear more clustered.

info_linkLength.description: How long should the links between nodes be? The shorter the links the more connected nodes will cluster together.

info_repulsionForce.description: How much should nodes repel each other? This will make disconnected parts more spread out.

info_centralForce.description: How much should nodes be attracted to the center? This will make the graph appear more dense and circular.

info_edgePruning.description: Edges with a length above this threshold will not be rendered, however they will still contribute to the simulation. This can help large tangled graphs look more organised. Hovering over a node will still display these links.

info_minNodeRadius.description: How small should the smallest nodes be? The smaller a node is the less it will attract other nodes.

info_maxNodeRadius.description: How large should the largest nodes be? Nodes are sized by how many links they have. The larger a node is the more it will attract other nodes. This can be used to create a good grouping around the most important nodes.

info_allowResizing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be resized

info_allowCollapsing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be collapsed

info_rightDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the right sidebar

info_leftDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the left sidebar

info_selector.description: CSS selector for an element. The feature will be placed relative to this element.

info_type.description: Will this feature be placed before, after, or inside (at the beggining or end).

info_featurePlacement.description: Where to place this feature on the page. (Relative to the selector)

info_sourcePath.description: The local path to the source .html file which will be included.

info_allowFoldingLists.description: Whether or not to allow lists to be folded

info_allowFoldingHeadings.description: Whether or not to allow headings to be folded

info_siteUrl.description: The url that this site will be hosted at

info_authorName.description: The name of the author of the site
